main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Macros


#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'
#define VERSION ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL
#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Typedefs


typedef int(* mode_entry_point_t) (void)

Functions


atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit)
bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)
int main (int argc, char *argv[])

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

This file implements the main entry point for the unified ascii-chat binary, which provides server, client, mirror, and discovery service functionality in a single executable.

The dispatcher now delegates ALL option parsing (including mode detection and binary-level options) to options_init(), then simply dispatches to the appropriate mode-specific entry point based on the detected mode.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

3.0

Fix: CMAKE_OSX_SYSROOT properly configured for macOS builds WebRTC: Pass CMAKE_OSX_SYSROOT to subprocess for Threads discovery Test: Running macOS builds with WebRTC Threads fix

Definition in file main.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'

Definition at line 93 of file main.c.

#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \


  if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \


    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \


    _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Definition at line 185 of file main.c.


185 #define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()                                                                                      \
186   do {                                                                                                                 \
187     static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \
188     if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \
189       NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \
190       _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \
191     }                                                                                                                  \
192   } while (0)

Function
  Documentation




atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO
  (g_should_exit )


Global flag indicating application should exit (used by all
  modes)


References g_should_exit.






int
  main (int argc, char * argv[])


Definition at line 467 of file main.c.


467                                  {
468   // Set global argc/argv for early argv inspection (e.g., in terminal.c)
469   g_argc = argc;
470   g_argv = argv;
471 
472   // Validate basic argument structure
473   if (argc < 1 || argv == NULL || argv[0] == NULL) {
474     return 1;
475   }
476 
477   // Show cursor early in case a previous session crashed with it hidden
478   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
479 
480   // Initialize the named registry for debugging (allows --debug-state to show registered synchronization primitives)
481 #ifndef NDEBUG
482   named_init();
483   // Initialize atomic operations debug tracking
484   debug_atomic_init();
485   // Register global atomics with descriptive names for debug sync state monitoring
486   REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS();
487   // Static atomics are now auto-registered via ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO macro at definition time
488   // Register all packet types from the packet_type_t enum
489   named_registry_register_packet_types();
490 #endif
491 
492   // Register the main thread IMMEDIATELY after named_init() to ensure it's available for all subsequent allocations
493 #ifndef NDEBUG
494   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD(asciichat_thread_self(), "main", NULL);
495   // Also save main thread ID for memory reporting (must be very early)
496   debug_sync_set_main_thread_id();
497 #endif
498 
499   // VERY FIRST: Scan for --color BEFORE ANY logging initialization
500   // This sets global flags that persist through cleanup, enabling --color to force colors
501   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
502     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--color=true") == 0) {
503       g_color_flag_passed = true;
504       g_color_flag_value = true;
505       break;
506     } else if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
507       g_color_flag_passed = true;
508       g_color_flag_value = strcmp(argv[i], "--color=false") != 0;
509       break;
510     }
511   }
512 
513   // SECOND: Scan for --grep BEFORE ANY logging initialization
514   // This ensures ALL logs (including from shared_init) can be filtered
515   // Supports multiple --grep patterns (ORed together)
516   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
517     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--grep") == 0) {
518       const char *pattern = argv[i + 1];
519       asciichat_error_t filter_result = grep_init(pattern);
520       if (filter_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
521         log_error("Invalid --grep pattern or invalid flags: \"%s\" - use /pattern/flags format (e.g., \"/query/ig\" or \"/literal/F\")", pattern);
522         return 1;
523       }
524       i++; // Skip the pattern argument
525     }
526   }
527 
528   // Detect terminal capabilities early so colored help output works
529   // Logging will be initialized by asciichat_shared_init() before options_init()
530   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
531   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
532   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
533 
534   // Warn if Release build was built from dirty working tree
535 #if ASCII_CHAT_GIT_IS_DIRTY
536   if (strcmp(ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, "Release") == 0) {
537     // Dirty git warning will be logged after logging initialization
538   }
539 #endif
540 
541   // Load color scheme early (from config files and CLI) before logging initialization
542   // This allows logging to use the correct colors from the start
543   options_colorscheme_init_early(argc, argv);
544 
545   // Parse --color-scheme from argv early to set logging colors for help output
546   const char *colorscheme_name = "pastel"; // default
547   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
548     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color-scheme") == 0) {
549       colorscheme_name = argv[i + 1];
550       break;
551     }
552   }
553 
554   // Load and apply colorscheme to logging BEFORE options_init() so help gets colors
555   color_scheme_t scheme;
556   if (colorscheme_load_builtin(colorscheme_name, &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
557     log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
558   } else {
559     // Fall back to default pastel if scheme not found
560     if (colorscheme_load_builtin("pastel", &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
561       log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
562     }
563   }
564 
565   // Initialize logging colors so they're ready for help output
566   log_init_colors();
567 
568   // EARLY PARSE: Find the mode position (first positional argument)
569   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
570   int mode_position = -1;
571   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
572     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
573       // Found a positional argument - this is the mode or session string
574       mode_position = i;
575       break;
576     }
577 
578     // Skip option and its argument if needed
579     // Check if this is an option that takes a required argument
580     const char *arg = argv[i];
581     const char *opt_name = arg;
582     if (arg[0] == '-') {
583       opt_name = arg + (arg[1] == '-' ? 2 : 1);
584     }
585 
586     // Handle --option=value format
587     if (strchr(opt_name, '=')) {
588       continue; // Value is part of this arg, no need to skip next
589     }
590 
591     // Options that take required arguments
592     if (strcmp(arg, "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "-L") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-level") == 0 ||
593         strcmp(arg, "--config") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--color-scheme") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-template") == 0) {
594       if (i + 1 < argc) {
595         i++; // Skip the argument value
596       }
597     }
598   }
599 
600   // EARLY PARSE: Determine mode from argv to know if this is client-like mode
601   // The first positional argument (after options) is the mode: client, server, mirror, discovery, acds
602   bool is_client_like_mode = false;
603   if (mode_position > 0) {
604     const char *first_arg = argv[mode_position];
605     if (strcmp(first_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "mirror") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "discovery") == 0) {
606       is_client_like_mode = true;
607     }
608   }
609 
610   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log file from argv (--log-file or -L)
611   // Must appear BEFORE the mode
612   // Generate default log path based on build type and mode
613   char default_log_path[PATH_MAX];
614   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_INVALID;
615   if (mode_position > 0) {
616     const char *mode_str = argv[mode_position];
617     // Convert mode string to enum
618     if (strcmp(mode_str, "server") == 0) {
619       detected_mode = MODE_SERVER;
620     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "client") == 0) {
621       detected_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
622     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "mirror") == 0) {
623       detected_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
624     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "acds") == 0 || strcmp(mode_str, "discovery-service") == 0) {
625       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
626     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "discovery") == 0) {
627       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
628     }
629   }
630   generate_default_log_path(detected_mode, default_log_path, sizeof(default_log_path));
631 
632   const char *log_file = default_log_path;
633   int max_search = (mode_position > 0) ? mode_position : argc;
634   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
635     if ((strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0)) {
636       log_file = argv[i + 1];
637       break;
638     }
639   }
640 
641   // EARLY PARSE: Check for --json (JSON logging format)
642   // If JSON format is enabled, we'll use the JSON filename during early init
643   // --json MUST appear BEFORE the mode
644   bool early_json_format = false;
645   if (mode_position > 0) {
646     for (int i = 1; i < mode_position; i++) {
647       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
648         early_json_format = true;
649         break;
650       }
651     }
652   } else {
653     // If no mode found, search entire argv
654     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
655       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
656         early_json_format = true;
657         break;
658       }
659     }
660   }
661 
662   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log template from argv (--log-template)
663   // Note: This is for format templates, different from --log-format which is the output format
664   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
665   const char *early_log_template = NULL;
666   bool early_log_template_console_only = false;
667   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
668     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-template") == 0) {
669       early_log_template = argv[i + 1];
670       break;
671     }
672   }
673   for (int i = 1; i < max_search; i++) {
674     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-format-console-only") == 0) {
675       early_log_template_console_only = true;
676       break;
677     }
678   }
679 
680   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
681   // This ensures options parsing can use properly configured logging with colors
682   // If JSON format is requested, don't write text logs to file
683   // All logs will be JSON formatted later once options_init() runs
684   const char *early_log_file = early_json_format ? NULL : log_file;
685   asciichat_error_t init_result = asciichat_shared_init(early_log_file, is_client_like_mode);
686   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
687     return init_result;
688   }
689 
690   // Route logs to stderr if stdout is piped (MUST happen early, before options_init logs)
691   // This keeps stdout clean for data output (e.g., --snapshot mode piped to file)
692   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
693     log_set_force_stderr(true);
694   }
695 
696   // Register cleanup of shared subsystems to run on normal exit
697   // Library code doesn't call atexit() - the application is responsible
698   // IMPORTANT: atexit handlers run in LIFO order - last registered runs first
699   // So register order (top to bottom) = execution order (bottom to top):
700   // 1. PCRE2 cleanup (runs last) - frees compiled regexes after all code stops using them
701   // 2. Path cleanup (runs 3rd) - now a no-op but kept for API compatibility
702   // 3. Shared destroy (runs 2nd) - frees all shared resources
703   // 4. Cursor show (runs 1st) - shows cursor before exit
704   (void)atexit(asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all);
705   (void)atexit(path_cleanup_thread_locals);
706   (void)atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy);
707   (void)atexit(on_exit_show_cursor);
708 
709   // SECRET: Check for --backtrace (debug builds only) BEFORE options_init()
710   // Prints a backtrace and exits immediately - useful for debugging hangs
711 #ifndef NDEBUG
712   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
713     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
714       log_info("=== Backtrace at startup ===");
715       backtrace_print_simple(0);
716       log_info("=== End Backtrace ===");
717       asciichat_shared_destroy();
718       return 0;
719     }
720   }
721 #endif
722 
723   // NOW parse all options - can use logging with colors!
724   asciichat_error_t options_result = options_init(argc, argv);
725   if (options_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
726     asciichat_error_context_t error_ctx;
727     if (HAS_ERRNO(&error_ctx)) {
728       log_error("Error: %s", error_ctx.context_message);
729     } else {
730       log_error("Error: Failed to initialize options (error code: %d)", options_result);
731     }
732 
733     // Clean up options state before exiting
734     options_state_destroy();
735     options_cleanup_schema();
736 
737     exit(options_result);
738   }
739 
740   // Get parsed options
741   const options_t *opts = options_get();
742   if (!opts) {
743     log_error("Error: Options not initialized");
744     return 1;
745   }
746 
747   // Determine final log file path (use mode-specific default from options if available)
748   // Determine output format early to decide on filename and logging strategy
749   bool use_json_logging = GET_OPTION(json);
750 
751   // Determine the log filename
752   // Check if user explicitly passed --log-file (not just the mode-specific default from options_init)
753   bool user_specified_log_file = false;
754   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
755     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0) {
756       user_specified_log_file = true;
757       break;
758     }
759   }
760 
761   const char *final_log_file = opts->log_file;
762   char json_filename_buf[256];
763 
764   if (use_json_logging) {
765     // When JSON logging is enabled, determine JSON output filename
766     if (user_specified_log_file) {
767       // User explicitly specified a log file - use it exactly for JSON output
768       SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
769     } else {
770       // Using default: replace .log with .json in the mode-specific default
771       // e.g., server.log -> server.json, mirror.log -> mirror.json, etc.
772       size_t len = strlen(final_log_file);
773       if (len > 4 && strcmp(&final_log_file[len - 4], ".log") == 0) {
774         // File ends with .log - replace with .json
775         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
776         // Replace .log with .json
777         asciichat_error_t strcpy_result = SAFE_STRCPY(&json_filename_buf[len - 4],
778                                                        sizeof(json_filename_buf) - (len - 4), ".json");
779         if (strcpy_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
780           log_error("Failed to write .json extension: %s", asciichat_error_string(strcpy_result));
781         }
782       } else {
783         // File doesn't end with .log - just append .json
784         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
785         strncat(json_filename_buf, ".json", sizeof(json_filename_buf) - strlen(json_filename_buf) - 1);
786       }
787     }
788     final_log_file = json_filename_buf;
789     // For JSON mode: Initialize logging without file (text will be disabled)
790     // We'll set up JSON output separately below
791     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
792   } else {
793     // Text logging mode: use the file from options (which is mode-specific default or user-specified)
794     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
795   }
796 
797   // Apply custom log template if specified (use early parsed value if available, otherwise use options)
798   const char *final_format = early_log_template ? early_log_template : GET_OPTION(log_template);
799   bool final_format_console_only =
800       early_log_template ? early_log_template_console_only : GET_OPTION(log_format_console_only);
801   if (final_format && final_format[0] != '\0') {
802     asciichat_error_t fmt_result = log_set_format(final_format, final_format_console_only);
803     if (fmt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
804       log_error("Failed to apply custom log format");
805     }
806   }
807 
808   // Configure JSON output if requested
809   if (use_json_logging) {
810     // Open the JSON file for output
811     int json_fd = platform_open("main_log_file", final_log_file, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
812     if (json_fd >= 0) {
813       log_set_json_output(json_fd);
814     } else {
815       // Failed to open JSON file - report error and exit
816       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open JSON output file: %s", final_log_file);
817       return ERROR_CONFIG;
818     }
819   }
820 
821   // Initialize colors now that logging is fully initialized
822   // This must happen after log_init() since log_init_colors() checks if g_log.initialized
823   log_init_colors();
824 
825   // Apply quiet mode - disables terminal output
826   // Status screen mode only disables terminal output if terminal is interactive
827   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
828   if (GET_OPTION(quiet) ||
829       (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive())) {
830     log_set_terminal_output(false);
831   }
832 
833   // Initialize palette based on command line options
834   const char *custom_chars = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : NULL;
835   if (apply_palette_config(GET_OPTION(palette_type), custom_chars) != 0) {
836     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to apply palette configuration");
837   }
838 
839   // Set quiet mode for memory debugging
840 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
841   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(GET_OPTION(quiet));
842 #endif
843 
844   // Truncate log if it's already too large
845   log_truncate_if_large();
846   fflush(stderr);
847 
848   // Handle --help and --version (these are detected and flagged by options_init)
849   // Terminal capabilities already initialized before options_init() at startup
850   if (opts->help) {
851     print_usage(opts->detected_mode);
852     fflush(NULL);
853     _Exit(0);
854   }
855 
856   if (opts->version) {
857     log_set_terminal_output(true);
858     action_show_version();
859     // action_show_version() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
860   }
861 
862 #ifndef NDEBUG
863   // Handle --debug-state (debug builds only)
864   // Sleep for specified time AFTER mode initialization so locks are created
865   if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT(debug_sync_state_time, opts) && opts->debug_sync_state_time > 0.0) {
866     log_info("Will print sync state after %f seconds", opts->debug_sync_state_time);
867   }
868 #endif
869 
870   // For server mode with status screen: disable terminal output only if interactive
871   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
872   // The status screen (when shown) will capture and display logs in its buffer instead
873   if (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && opts->status_screen && terminal_is_interactive()) {
874     log_set_terminal_output(false);
875   }
876 
877   log_dev("Logging initialized to %s", final_log_file);
878 
879   // Note: We do NOT auto-disable colors when stdout appears to be piped, because:
880   // 1. Tools like ripgrep can display ANSI colors when piped
881   // 2. Sandboxed/containerized environments may report false positives for isatty()
882   // 3. Users can explicitly disable colors with --color=false if needed
883   // Color behavior is now fully controlled by --color and --color-mode options.
884 
885 #ifndef NDEBUG
886   // Initialize lock debugging system after logging is fully set up
887   log_debug("Initializing lock debug system...");
888   int debug_sync_result = debug_sync_init();
889   if (debug_sync_result != 0) {
890     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync system initialization failed");
891     FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Debug sync system initialization failed");
892   }
893   log_debug("Debug sync system initialized successfully");
894 
895   // Initialize memory debug system
896   // TEMPORARILY DISABLED: these calls hang in non-interactive mode
897   // log_debug("Initializing memory debug system...");
898   // int debug_memory_result = debug_memory_thread_init();
899   // if (debug_memory_result != 0) {
900   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug system initialization failed");
901   //   FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Memory debug system initialization failed");
902   // }
903   // log_debug("Memory debug system initialized successfully");
904 
905   // Start memory debug (now just prints report directly)
906   // if (debug_memory_thread_start() != 0) {
907   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug startup failed");
908   //   FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Memory debug startup failed");
909   // }
910 
911 #ifndef _WIN32
912   // Unblock SIGUSR1 and SIGUSR2 at process level to ensure delivery
913   sigset_t set;
914   sigemptyset(&set);
915   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR1);
916   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR2);
917   sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
918 
919   // Register SIGUSR1 to trigger synchronization debugging output in all modes
920   signal_handler_t old_usr1 = platform_signal(SIGUSR1, common_handle_sigusr1);
921   if (old_usr1 == SIG_ERR) {
922     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR1 handler");
923   } else {
924     log_debug("SIGUSR1 handler registered successfully");
925   }
926 
927   // Register SIGUSR2 to trigger memory report in all modes
928   signal_handler_t old_usr2 = platform_signal(SIGUSR2, common_handle_sigusr2);
929   if (old_usr2 == SIG_ERR) {
930     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR2 handler");
931   } else {
932     log_debug("SIGUSR2 handler registered successfully");
933   }
934 #endif
935 #endif
936 
937   if (opts->fps > 0) {
938     if (opts->fps < 1 || opts->fps > 144) {
939       log_warn("FPS value %d out of range (1-144), using default", opts->fps);
940     } else {
941       log_debug("FPS set from command line: %d", opts->fps);
942     }
943   }
944 
945   // Automatic update check at startup (once per week maximum)
946   if (!GET_OPTION(no_check_update)) {
947     update_check_result_t update_result;
948     asciichat_error_t update_err = update_check_startup(&update_result);
949     if (update_err == ASCIICHAT_OK && update_result.update_available) {
950       char notification[1024];
951       update_check_format_notification(&update_result, notification, sizeof(notification));
952       log_info("%s", notification);
953 
954       // Set update notification for splash/status screens
955       splash_set_update_notification(notification);
956     }
957   }
958 
959   // Set up global signal handlers BEFORE mode dispatch
960   // All modes use the same centralized exit mechanism
961   setup_signal_handlers();
962 
963   // Register application callbacks so lib code can check exit flags
964   // This connects the render loop's should_exit check to signal_exit()
965   static const app_callbacks_t app_callbacks = {
966       .should_exit = should_exit,
967       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
968       .platform_get_monotonic_time_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us,
969       .platform_sleep_ms = platform_sleep_ms,
970   };
971   app_callbacks_register(&app_callbacks);
972 
973   // Register shutdown callback so splash thread and other code can check for exit
974   shutdown_register_callback((shutdown_check_fn)should_exit);
975 
976 #ifndef NDEBUG
977   // Start debug threads now, after initialization but before mode entry
978   // This avoids lock contention during critical initialization phase
979   // Note: debug_sync_start_thread() and debug_memory_thread_start() now use
980   // direct pthread calls instead of mutex_init/cond_init to avoid named registry
981   // deadlock during thread startup
982   if (debug_sync_start_thread() != 0) {
983     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync thread startup failed");
984     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Debug sync thread startup failed");
985   }
986   log_debug("Debug sync thread started");
987 
988 #endif
989 
990   // Find and dispatch to mode entry point
991   const mode_descriptor_t *mode = find_mode(opts->detected_mode);
992   if (!mode) {
993     log_error("Error: Mode not found for detected_mode=%d", opts->detected_mode);
994     return 1;
995   }
996 
997   // Call the mode-specific entry point
998   // Mode entry points use options_get() to access parsed options
999   int exit_code = mode->entry_point();
1000 
1001   // Named registry cleanup is handled by asciichat_shared_destroy()
1002   // (called via atexit handler), which runs AFTER the memory report
1003 
1004   if (exit_code == ERROR_USAGE) {
1005     exit(ERROR_USAGE);
1006   }
1007 
1008   return exit_code;
1009 }






References action_show_version(),
    app_callbacks_register(), apply_color_mode_override(),
    apply_palette_config(), ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(),
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), asciichat_shared_init(),
    asciichat_thread_self(), backtrace_print_simple(),
    colorscheme_load_builtin(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    debug_atomic_init(), debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(),
    debug_sync_init(), debug_sync_set_main_thread_id(),
    debug_sync_start_thread(),
    options_state::debug_sync_state_time,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), options_state::detected_mode,
    mode_descriptor_t::entry_point, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, ERROR_THREAD, ERROR_USAGE,
    FATAL, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE, options_state::fps,
    g_argc, g_argv, g_color_flag_passed,
    g_color_flag_value, GET_OPTION, grep_init(),
    HAS_ERRNO, options_state::help, IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT,
    log_debug, log_dev, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_file, options_state::log_file, log_info,
    log_init_colors(), log_redetect_terminal_capabilities(),
    log_set_color_scheme(), log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_format(), log_set_json_output(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_truncate_if_large(),
    log_warn, MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID, MODE_MIRROR,
    MODE_SERVER, named_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD,
    named_registry_register_packet_types(),
    options_cleanup_schema(), options_colorscheme_init_early(),
    options_get(), options_init(), options_state_destroy(),
    options_state::palette_custom,
    options_state::palette_custom_set,
    path_cleanup_thread_locals(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_open(),
    platform_signal(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS, SAFE_STRCPY, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    SET_ERRNO, setup_signal_handlers(), should_exit(),
    shutdown_register_callback(), signal_exit(),
    splash_set_update_notification(),
    options_state::status_screen, terminal_cursor_show(),
    terminal_is_interactive(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    update_check_result_t::update_available,
    update_check_format_notification(), update_check_startup(),
    and options_state::version.






void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 222 of file main.c.


222                                               {
223   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
224 }










void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 341 of file main.c.


341                                  {
342   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
343 
344 #ifndef _WIN32
345   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
346       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
347       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
348       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
349   };
350   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
351   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
352 #endif
353 }






References log_debug,
    platform_register_signal_handlers(), and
    platform_set_console_ctrl_handler().


Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 206 of file main.c.


206                        {
207   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
208 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 210 of file main.c.


210                        {
211   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
212   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
213   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
214   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
215   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
216   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
217   if (cb) {
218     cb();
219   }
220 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
